title: Boat Rental in Buenos Aires: Costs and What to Know 2026
html:
Renting a yacht in Buenos Aires means cruising the broad Rio de la Plata from marinas such as Puerto Madero and the Dique 4 basin, with skippered day charters typically priced from around EUR 350 to EUR 1,200 depending on the boat. BoatHire24 lists verified vessels across the city waterfront.

Where to set off in Buenos Aires

Most charters in Buenos Aires leave from the river basins close to the city centre or from the green delta a short drive north. The water here is the wide, brackish Rio de la Plata rather than open sea, so the feel is calm and broad rather than coastal.

  • Puerto Madero: the regenerated dock district beside the financial centre, with restored basins and the Puente de la Mujer footbridge.
  • Dique 4: a popular departure basin within Puerto Madero for day trips and sunset cruises.
  • Tigre and the Parana Delta: a labyrinth of channels and wooded islands roughly 30 kilometres north of the centre.
  • San Isidro: a leafy riverside suburb with a long-established yacht club and sheltered moorings.
  • Olivos: a calm bay just north of the city, handy for shorter river outings.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Buenos Aires?

Expect roughly EUR 350 for a half-day on a small motorboat and EUR 800 to EUR 1,200 for a crewed yacht across a full day. Prices shift with season, boat size and whether a skipper and fuel are included, so always read the listing terms before you confirm. You can rent a boat in the city through transparent listings when you want boat rental Buenos Aires options compared side by side.

  • Shared river tour: from about EUR 25 to EUR 45 per person for a couple of hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day: from around EUR 350 for up to six guests.
  • Catamaran full-day: from roughly EUR 700 for a wider, stable deck.
  • Crewed yacht: from about EUR 900 for a full day with skipper.

Which boat to rent in Buenos Aires

The right boat depends on your group size and whether you want a quick spin past Puerto Madero or a full day drifting through the delta. The river is shallow and busy in places, so capable hulls and a local skipper make the day easier.

  1. Open motorboat: ideal for short city-front spins and small groups, seats up to 6, from EUR 350.
  2. Motor yacht: a fine choice for comfort and shade across a long day, holding up to 10 guests, from EUR 800. The the Azimut 58 listing shows the sort of flybridge cruiser that suits this brackish, open expanse of water.
  3. Catamaran: stable and roomy for families, comfortable for up to 12, from EUR 700.
  4. Sailing yacht: rewarding when the river breeze fills in, seats up to 8, from EUR 600.
  5. RIB or tender: nimble for delta channels and quick transfers, seats up to 5, from EUR 250.

When is the best season, and do you need a licence?

The high season runs from November to March, the southern-hemisphere summer, and a skipper handles the licence so you do not need one. Argentina applies the usual principle that the person at the helm holds the relevant qualification, and on a crewed charter that is the operator. Keep an eye on the local sudestada, a persistent south-easterly that can push river levels up and stir short, choppy waves.

For the calmest river light in Buenos Aires, book a December or January morning before the afternoon sudestada builds; the operator confirms exact licence and safety requirements for your chosen boat when you book.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 350 half-day to EUR 1,200 full crewed day.
  • Peak months: November to March.
  • Typical trip length: 3 to 8 hours.
  • Guest capacity: commonly 6 to 12 aboard day boats.
  • Currency: euro pricing shown for comparison, settled locally where required.
  • Distance: Tigre sits roughly 16 nautical miles up-river from Puerto Madero.

Questions about boat rental in Buenos Aires

Is the Rio de la Plata good for a day on the water?

The river is wide, calm and famously brown with sediment, which makes it well suited to relaxed cruising and sightseeing rather than swimming or snorkelling, so plan a sociable deck day rather than a beach-style outing.

Can beginners join a charter without experience?

Beginners are welcome on crewed charters because a professional skipper handles navigation, the busy basins and the river traffic, leaving guests free to relax, enjoy the skyline and learn a little as the day unfolds.

How far is the Parana Delta from the city?

The delta around Tigre lies roughly 30 kilometres north of central Buenos Aires by road, and reaching its wooded channels by boat is itself a scenic part of the trip through quiet, tree-lined waterways.

What should guests bring aboard?

Pack sun protection, a light layer for breezy afternoons and flat-soled shoes, since the river sun is strong in summer and the sudestada wind can make the open deck noticeably cooler later in the day.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Buenos Aires or list your own, and in 2026 the city waterfront remains an easy, scenic place to spend a day afloat. Boat owners along the Rio de la Plata can also become a host and reach travellers searching for verified river charters.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Buenos Aires and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
